pattern: Count internal seam alignments with a hash map of running edge positions; the line crossing the fewest bricks passes through the most-shared seam, so the answer is total rows minus the largest seam frequency.
hints: A vertical line crosses a brick only when it does not coincide with a gap between two bricks in that row.
For each row, accumulate the prefix widths to find the column positions where gaps occur, ignoring the far right edge.
Tally how many rows share each gap position; the best line goes through the most popular gap, crossing rows minus that count.

edge_inputs.description: single full-width brick per row leaves no internal seam
one row, best line uses any internal seam
perfectly aligned seams cross zero bricks
no internal seam aligns across rows

stress.description: random rows each summing to a fixed total width
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = rng.randint(1, max(1, size // 4))
    total = rng.randint(2, 60)
    wall = []
    for _ in range(rows):
        remaining = total
        row = []
        while remaining > 0:
            w = rng.randint(1, remaining)
            row.append(w)
            remaining -= w
        wall.append(row)
    return [wall]
stress.note: all rows share the same total width as required
